title: DEXA Scan Reveals Side Effects of Red Meat & Fruit

chunks.title: Introduction to DEXA Scan and Body Composition
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses their experience with an animal-based diet over five years and introduces the upcoming DEXA scan to analyze body fat, muscle mass, and bone density. They briefly explain what the DEXA scan measures and its significance.
chunks.transcript: I've been eating an animal- based diet for more than 5 years what's my body fat how much visceral fat what's my bone density what are my muscles and fat look like in my body I'm going to go get a dexa scan and put this diet to the test let's see what I got nice to meet you

chunks.title: Body Fat Percentage and Health Risks
chunks.summary: During the scan, the technician provides insights on the speaker's body fat percentage of 10.5%, indicating it falls within the normal range with reduced health risks. They discuss variations in body fat percentages among different individuals based on factors like activity level and gender.
chunks.transcript: Paul nice to meet you so this front page will include the summary for your whole body which includes your total body fat percentage your weight taken from the scale built into the machine and the totals for fat tissue and lean tissue in your body as of today now lean tissue will include everything that is not fat and Bones so it's more than just muscle mass there's water pendant ligaments organs will go in there if you had any food or liquids consumed during this morning then that would go into those totals as well says total body fat is 10.5 MH so you're in the normal category where we see a decrease risk um of different health conditions how many people do you see that have a body fat of 10.5% not many for most men that I've seen in my personal experience like this is not science-based information um they tend to vary from Maybe to 22 mhm yeah and then that's on the people that have shared that they do engage in sports and physical activities um for people that are just starting to become more active um I tend to see a little bit higher percentage but then again the body fat percentage itself can vary so much from person to person and what about for women uh for women we tend to be a little bit higher in percentages just because we have different Necessities when it comes to hormonal differences it's interesting because if I were 11 .5% more fat I wouldn't think that wouldn't be a normal body fat to me but I guess you know 18 to 49 years old I'm 46 years old less than 24.5 yeah the ranges seem a little bit LAX to me but well we're not addressing athleticism or Aesthetics or a specific um preference for body fat percentage we're just addressing the risks we've seen increase

chunks.title: Visceral Fat Analysis
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to visceral fat, where the technician informs the speaker about their visceral fat measurement, indicating it’s on the lower end of the spectrum. They discuss the health risks associated with high visceral fat and the importance of monitoring changes over time.
chunks.transcript: page we have your visceral fat scan since this is the type of fat tissue that being stored around your organs and through research we've seen a closer relationship to increasing the risk for heart disease stroke diabetes hypertension amongst other conditions we want to keep TR track of this one the most so it's pretty hard to give you a number to aim for what we look into is the tendency throughout time to see if you're if you've increased drastically in a short period of time which would change the conditions at which your organs are working or if you're decreasing or maintaining okay so visceral fat3 pounds it's on the lower end of what I tend to see in dexas scans yeah okay is this does the visceral fat give you any indication of the amount of fat in your liver this technology will not tell you specifically in around which organ it's being stored um so it's just in general below the abdominal wall what's being stored there so right now this is the amount in pounds that it's being stored around your organs which if you want a point of reference it's on the lower end of what I tend to see but then again that's not science-based

chunks.title: Fat Distribution and Android-Gynoid Ratio
chunks.summary: The technician explains the Android-to-Gynoid (AG) ratio, which measures fat distribution in the body. They elaborate on the significance of understanding fat distribution in relation to health risks and how individual genetics influence these factors.
chunks.transcript: comparison of region fat percentage between your Android region and the gynoid region the Android region is from below the rib cage to that lower end of the trunk so that line that you see right on top of your pelvis and the gynoid is going to be this triangle right here then incorporates part of your hips glutes and thighs we include this as a secondary measurement to understand fat distribution in your body and have a rough idea of what type of risk do we see based on that distribution doctors used to take the circumference measurements around your waist and your hips and get a ratio from that this is a modern version of that where we're comparing the percentages that you see on the following page here which is six and through 13.2 right from those regions for you and then that's where we get that AG ratio which optimally we want to aim for anything lower below 1.0 but then again genetically where we store fat and where we burn it it's going to vary a lot so that's why it's a secondary measurement

chunks.title: Muscle Balance and Extremity Analysis
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses their muscle mass differences between their arms and legs, revealing a slight imbalance. The technician shares methods to address these imbalances through targeted exercises to ensure equal strength development on both sides.
chunks.transcript: as well where we have both of your arms in the top table and then the comparison between your right right and left side at the bottom where we can address the amount of lean tissu stored in each side so that we can try to see if there's a noticeable difference where we may see a muscle imbalance and therefore an increased risk of a postural problem mhm but right now with a one pound difference from lean tissue from your right side to your left side that's still within what we tend to see it's interesting so there's a difference of one pound of muscle between my right arm and my left arm so I'm right-handed that's just coming from doing things with my right arm more could be yes interesting mhm so I need to do more things with my left hand what I tend to do is my single arm exercises I'll start them out with my lace dominant side and then try to push that one close to failure and then just do the same amount of reps and sets with my right side because if I tend to start with the right by the last few reps on my set for my left side then I'm already struggling

chunks.title: Bone Density Assessment and Comparison
chunks.summary: The DEXA scan reports the speaker's bone density, indicating they are in the normal range compared to their age group. Discussion includes how different athletic disciplines might influence bone density and highlights the importance of weight-bearing exercises.
chunks.transcript: density so here we have two scores we have your t score and the zcore the t score is the one that we use to screen if there's a possibility of suffering from oopen and osis where we're comparing the analysis of your full skeleton from your skull to your toes um and comparing it to a healthy 30-year-old so that way we can see if there's a noticeable loss of bone tissue where we chunks.title: Resting Metabolic Rate Insight
chunks.summary: The technician explains how the resting metabolic rate (RMR) is calculated and its implications for daily caloric needs. The discussion addresses the variability in RMR calculations and the potential inaccuracies of standard equations for active individuals.
chunks.transcript: estimated with the haris benedic equation so we're assuming that your metabolism works at an average rate but it could be faster or slower so just know that there could be some variance for most people it can go up to 200 calories plus or minus um but this is what's estimated for you to burn at rest if you were just to lay down for 24 hours without any physical activities therefore we're um including a chart to give you a rough idea of how much you're actually burning per day assuming that you're not just laying down for 24 hours does that make sense yeah so it says chunks.title: Dietary Habits and Nutritional Insights
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their daily caloric intake and macronutrient distribution, emphasizing the importance of high-quality foods in their diet. They reflect on the differences between animal-based and plant-based proteins and how their eating patterns align with their fitness goals.
chunks.transcript: yeah so that's based on the Institute of medicine to stay within those ranges it's not necessarily for you to follow those some people like to challenge those with maybe keto diets uh um are a little bit more into like high fat high protein but overall that's what what's advised to stay within yeah I'm not into keto diets at all I probably do I probably do 300 plus grams of carbohydrates a day 180 grams of fat and probably off top of my head maybe about 180 grams of protein and like I said when I do chronometer I get over 33,000 to 3500 calories a day okay do you ever talk to people I guess would your dietitians talk to people about the differences between the quality of protein between animal foods and plant Foods um I'm not necessarily sure if they discussed that subject okay cool yeah in summary 10.5% body fat that's about what I expected I'm pretty lean I'm not dieting I eat three meals a day I eat 3500 calories a day I don't fast I don't do intermittent fasting and for my activity I do surf a couple hours a day so what's interesting for me about this dexa is that my visceral fat is very low 3 F pounds many people will say eating honey eating fruit eating fructose that causes liver fat well I've essentially got a very very low amount of visceral fat I don't have any significant amount of fat in my Android region as you guys heard in that video in my viscera there's very very small amount of fat around my organs anyone arguing that I have fatty liver or fatty organs or that eating fruit and honey is going to cause visceral fat I mean that's pretty much the proof for you right there like there's there's really nothing significant there I think

chunks.title: Conclusion and Personal Reflection
chunks.summary: In the conclusion, the speaker reflects on the DEXA scan results and what they imply about their health and fitness. They express confidence in their dietary choices and outline future goals for improving bone density while maintaining their regimen.
chunks.transcript: evidence that eating fruit eating honey will not cause you to gain weight will not cause you to gain visceral fat will not cause you to get fat around your liver at all so a deex scan is kind of interesting because it means for me in my training that I should focus on the left side a little more meaning maybe I should start my sets on the left side make sure I'm doing a good not gaining weight maintaining 10.5% body fat so maybe if you get a DEX that gives you a sense of where you want to be with your calories I don't really believe counting calories is that valuable in
